title: Unknown angle problems (with algebra)
description: Practice setting up algebraic equations to solve unknown angle problems.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$ in the diagram below.**



[[☃ image 1]]

$x=$ [[☃ input-number 3]] $^\circ$
hints:
The angle with measure $4x^\circ$ and the angles with measures $15^\circ$ and $45^\circ$ combined are vertical angles. Vertical angles are congruent.

[[☃ image 1]]

###Writing an equation


Since vertical angles are congruent, we can set their measures equal to each other.

[[☃ image 1]]

 $\blueD{4x^\circ} = \goldD{15^\circ}+ \greenD{45^\circ}$

options.alt: Two lines intersect forming angles. One of the angles is marked as 4 X degrees. Another angle opposite to this is divided by a ray into two parts. In clockwise order, the angles are marked as 45 degrees and 15 degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$ in the diagram below.**



[[☃ image 1]]

$x=$ [[☃ input-number 3]] $^\circ$
hints:
The angle with measure $150^\circ$ and the angles with measures $2x^\circ$ and $50^\circ$ combined are vertical angles. Vertical angles are congruent.

[[☃ image 1]]

###Writing an equation


Since vertical angles are congruent, we can set their measures equal to each other.

[[☃ image 1]]

 $\blueD{150^\circ} = \goldD{2x^\circ}+ \greenD{50^\circ}$

options.alt: Two lines intersect forming angles. One of the angles is marked and labeled as 150 degrees. Another angle opposite to this is divided by a ray into two parts. In clockwise order, the angles are marked as 50 degrees and 2 X degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$ in the diagram below.**



[[☃ image 1]]

$x=$ [[☃ input-number 3]] $^\circ$
hints:
The angles shown are vertical angles. Vertical angles are congruent.

[[☃ image 1]]

###Writing an equation


Since vertical angles are congruent, we can set their measures equal to each other.

[[☃ image 1]]

 $\blueD{60^\circ} = \goldD{(x+45)^\circ}$

options.alt: Two lines intersect forming angles. One of the angles is marked and labeled as 60 degrees. Another angle opposite to this is marked and labeled as parenthesis open x plus 45 parenthesis closed degrees. The angle between these two is divided by a ray into two parts.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$ in the diagram below.**



[[☃ image 1]]

$x=$ [[☃ input-number 3]] $^\circ$
hints:
The angles shown are vertical angles. Vertical angles are congruent.

[[☃ image 1]]

###Writing an equation


Since vertical angles are congruent, we can set their measures equal to each other.

[[☃ image 1]]

 $\blueD{110^\circ} = \goldD{(8x+30)^\circ}$

options.alt: Two lines intersect forming angles. One of the angles is marked as 110 degrees. Another angle opposite to this is marked as parenthesis open 8 X plus 30 parenthesis closed degrees. The angle between these two is divided by a ray into two parts.

options.alt: Two lines intersect forming angles. One of the angles is marked as 60 degrees. Another angle opposite to this is marked as parenthesis open x plus 40 parenthesis closed degrees.

[[☃ image 1]]

hints:
The angles shown are vertical angles. Vertical angles are congruent.

[[☃ image 1]]

###Writing an equation

Since vertical angles are congruent, we can set their measures equal to each other.

[[☃ image 1]]

 $\blueD{150^\circ} = \goldD{(4x+50)^\circ}$

options.alt: Two lines intersect forming angles. One of the angles is marked 150 degrees. Another angle opposite to this is marked as parenthesis open 4 X plus 50 parenthesis closed degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$ in the diagram below.**



[[☃ image 1]]

$x=$ [[☃ input-number 3]] $^\circ$
hints:
The angles shown are vertical angles. Vertical angles are congruent.

[[☃ image 1]]

###Writing an equation

Since vertical angles are congruent, we can set their measures equal to each other.

[[☃ image 1]]

 $\blueD{3x^\circ} = \goldD{120^\circ}$

options.alt: Two lines intersect forming angles. One of the angles is marked as 120 degrees. Another angle opposite to this is marked as 3 X degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$ in the diagram below.**



[[☃ image 1]]

$x=$ [[☃ input-number 3]] $^\circ$
hints:
###Writing an equation

All three angles form a straight line, so have a sum of $180^\circ$. Since one of the angles is $90^\circ$, the two remaining angles are complementary and have a sum of $90^\circ$.


[[☃ image 1]]

$
\blueD{5x^\circ} + \maroonD{(x + 54)^\circ} = 90^\circ$.

options.alt: Two rays divide a straight angle into three parts labeled as 5 X degrees, parenthesis open x plus 54 parenthesis closed degrees and a right angle.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$ in the diagram below.**



[[☃ image 1]]

$x=$ [[☃ input-number 3]] $^\circ$
hints:
###Writing an equation

All three angles form a straight line, so have a sum of $180^\circ$. Since one of the angles is $90^\circ$, the two remaining angles are complementary and  have a sum of $90^\circ$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{20^\circ} + \maroonD{(6x -2)^\circ}= 90^\circ$.

options.alt: Two rays divide a straight angle into three parts. In clockwise order, the angles are marked as 20 degrees, parenthesis open 6 times x minus 2 parentheses closed degrees, and a right angle.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$ in the diagram below.**



[[☃ image 1]]

$x=$ [[☃ input-number 3]] $^\circ$
hints:
###Writing an equation


The two angles together are complementary, which means they add up to $90^\circ$.  

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{x^\circ}+ \maroonD{(3x + 10)^\circ} = 90^\circ$.

options.alt: A ray divides a diagonally oriented right angle into two parts. In clockwise order, the angles are marked as x degrees and parenthesis open 3 X plus 10 parenthesis closed degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$ in the diagram below.**



[[☃ image 1]]

$x=$ [[☃ input-number 3]] $^\circ$
hints:
###Writing an equation


The two angles together are complementary, which means they add up to $90^\circ$.  

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{35^\circ}+ \maroonD{5x^\circ} = 90^\circ$.

options.alt: A ray divides a diagonally oriented right angle into two parts labeled as 35 degrees and 5 X degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$ in the diagram below.**




[[☃ image 1]]

$x=$ [[☃ input-number 3]] $^\circ$
hints:
###Writing an equation


The two angles together are complementary, which means they add up to $90^\circ$.  

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{(x+42)^\circ}+ \maroonD{3x^\circ} = 90^\circ$.

options.alt: A line divides a right angle into two parts. In clockwise order, the angles are marked as parenthesis open x plus 42 parenthesis closed degrees and 3 X degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$ in the diagram below.**



[[☃ image 1]]

$x=$ [[☃ input-number 3]] $^\circ$
hints:
###Writing an equation


The two angles together are complementary, which means they add up to $90^\circ$.  

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{8x^\circ}+ \maroonD{50^\circ} = 90^\circ$.

options.alt: A line divides a right angle into two angles 8 X degrees and 50 degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$ in the diagram below.**



[[☃ image 1]]

$x=$ [[☃ input-number 3]] $^\circ$
hints:
###Writing an equation


The three angles together are complementary, which means they add up to $90^\circ$.  

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{x^\circ}+ \maroonD{2x^\circ} +\goldD{(x + 10)^\circ}= 90^\circ$.

options.alt: Two lines divide a right angle into three parts labeled as x degrees, 2 X degrees, and parenthesis open x plus 10 parenthesis closed degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$ in the diagram below.**





[[☃ image 1]]

$x=$ [[☃ input-number 3]] $^\circ$
hints:
###Writing an equation


All three angles together are supplementary, which means they add up to $180^\circ$.  

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{(x+12^\circ)} +\maroonD{ 100^\circ}+ \goldD{x^\circ} = 180^\circ$

options.alt: Three lines intersect forming angles. In clockwise order, the angles are marked as parenthesis open x plus 12 parenthesis closed degrees, 100 degrees, and x degrees. The three angles line adjacent to each other in a straight line.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$ in the diagram below.**



[[☃ image 1]]

$x=$ [[☃ input-number 3]] $^\circ$
hints:
###Writing an equation


All three angles together are supplementary, which means they add up to $180^\circ$.  

[[☃ image 1]]



 $\blueD{3x^\circ} + \maroonD{80^\circ}+ \goldD{2x^\circ} = 180^\circ$

options.alt: Two rays divide a straight angle into three parts labeled 3 X degrees, 80 degrees, an 2 X degrees, from left to right, respectively.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$ in the diagram below.**



[[☃ image 1]]

$x=$ [[☃ input-number 3]] $^\circ$
hints:
###Writing an equation


The two  angles together are supplementary, which means they add up to $180^\circ$.  

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{(2x + 45)^\circ}+ \goldD{x^\circ} = 180^\circ$

options.alt: A ray divides a straight angle into two parts labeled as parenthesis open 2 X plus 45 parenthesis closed degrees and x degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$ in the diagram below.**

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{3x^\circ}+ \goldD{75^\circ} = 180^\circ$

options.alt: Two lines intersect forming angles. One of the angles is marked as 3 X degrees. Another angle adjacent to this is marked as 75 degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$ in the diagram below.**





[[☃ image 1]]

$x=$ [[☃ input-number 3]] $^\circ$
hints:
###Writing an equation


All three angles together are supplementary, which means they add up to $180^\circ$.  

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{ x^\circ} + \maroonD{100^\circ}+\goldD{3 x^\circ} = 180^\circ$

options.alt: Two rays divide a straight angle into three parts. In clockwise order, the angles are marked as 3 X degrees, 100 degrees, and x degrees. The angles are oriented below the straight line.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$ in the diagram below.**




[[☃ image 1]]

$x=$ [[☃ input-number 3]] $^\circ$
hints:
###Writing an equation


All three angles together are supplementary, which means they add up to $180^\circ$.  

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{ 40^\circ} + \maroonD{(2x + 30)^\circ}+\goldD{40^\circ} = 180^\circ$

options.alt: Two lines divide a straight angle into three parts. In clockwise order, the angles are labeled as 40 degrees, parenthesis open 2 times x plus 30 parenthesis closed degrees and 40 degrees. The two lines join together to form a triangle.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$ in the diagram below.**




[[☃ image 1]]

$x=$ [[☃ input-number 3]] $^\circ$
hints:
###Writing an equation

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{ x^\circ} + \maroonD{100^\circ}+\goldD{x^\circ} = 180^\circ$

options.alt: Two lines divide a straight angle into three parts. In clockwise order, the angles are marked and labeled as x degrees, 100 degrees and x degrees. The two lines join together to form a triangle.
